title: net_if_set_link_addr cast discards const - silent UB if Zephyr writes to it
description:
File: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:315

net_if_set_link_addr(iface, (uint8_t *)eui64, ...) casts away const from the EUI-64 pointer returned by lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64().

The EUI-64 is stored in static lora_state.eui64. If Zephyr's net_if code ever writes to this buffer (to set a user-requested address, for example), it would corrupt lora_state.eui64 without any warning.

Trigger: Future Zephyr API change allows setting link address through net_if.

Bad behavior: Lora_state.eui64 silently corrupted; subsequent calls to lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() return modified value.

Fix: Copy EUI-64 to a local buffer before passing to net_if_set_link_addr, or add a SECURITY comment acknowledging the const-discard is intentional and safe given current Zephyr API.
